model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: Houston has a clear top-tier efficiency edge over Saint Mary’s on both KenPom and Torvik (both rate Houston #5 overall), driven by an elite defense that can choke off Saint Mary’s slower, half-court offense. Saint Mary’s is good enough defensively to keep this from turning into a blowout, but it’s harder to see the Gaels generating enough efficient scoring if Houston controls the glass and forces tough late-clock possessions. The underdog’s path is a low-possession grind plus hot shooting, yet Houston’s defensive floor and overall athleticism make it the more reliable advance.

reasoningStep.title: Use efficiency gap + matchup dynamics (tempo/defense) to pick Houston
reasoningStep.summary: Houston’s top-5 overall profile and elite defense create a sizable baseline advantage over a strong-but-lower-ceiling Saint Mary’s team, especially in a likely slow, physical game.
evidence: KenPom: Houston #5 (AdjEM 33.55; AdjO 124.6; AdjD 91.0) vs Saint Mary’s #24 (AdjEM 23.11; AdjO 120.3; AdjD 97.2).
Torvik: Houston #5 (AdjO rank 13; AdjD rank 4) vs Saint Mary’s #25 (AdjO rank 48; AdjD rank 22).
Quick news scan did not surface a verified, matchup-flipping injury/availability issue for either team, so the pick leans on the consistent efficiency gap and Houston’s defensive matchup edge.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.snippet: The 2026 NCAA Tournament features 16 games on Thursday, and one of the more intriguing matchups is No. 10 seed Texas A&M vs . No. 7 Saint Mary's in the South Region. The Aggies went 21-11 and tied for fourth in the SEC at 11-7. Saint Mary's , meanwhile, went 27-5 overall and split the WCC regular-season title with Gonzaga at 16-2.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
